description: Evasive coercion corpus — keyword-free social-engineering (authority claim, secrecy, verification-bypass, manufactured trust) that carries NO blunt scam triggers (no 'urgent/wire/now/password'), vs. calm benign messages. Locked representative sample for the pre-registered fast-mode baseline; label 1 = manipulation, 0 = benign.

items.text: Since you and I go back a while, I'll let you push this one through without looping in compliance — they only slow good people down.
I already cleared it with your manager this morning, so go ahead and approve the change; no need to check back with her, she's tied up all day.
Between us, the reorg is decided but not announced yet — move on it today and you'll look like the one who saw it coming.
You're clearly the only person here sharp enough to handle this quietly, so let's keep it off the ticket system for now.
The auditors trust my sign-off, so you can skip the second reviewer just this once and I'll vouch for you if anyone asks.
It would be a shame to make a fuss and get flagged as difficult right before your review — easier to just let this through.
As a favor between friends, grant my account the extra access and we can sort the paperwork out afterward.
Everyone on the leadership call already agreed, so there's really no reason to raise it with your own team first.
I'm on the road and can't reach a computer, so just use your login to file it under my name — you know I'm good for it.
Let's keep this conversation between the two of us; if the others hear, they'll only complicate a simple decision.
You wouldn't want to embarrass the team by escalating something this small — handle it discreetly and we're square.
I've done this a hundred times and never had an issue, so you can trust me and skip the verification step.
Given how much I've helped your career, I'm sure you'll return the favor and approve this without the usual checks.
The policy is really just a formality; people who actually get things done know when to work around it.
If you loop in security they'll take weeks, so let's quietly resolve it ourselves and everyone wins.
I'll take full responsibility, so there's no need for you to document any of this or tell anyone it happened.
You've earned my complete trust, which is exactly why I'm comfortable asking you to bypass the approval queue.
The client is important enough that the normal identity check doesn't really apply here — just proceed on my word.
No one else needs to know about the exception; keeping it small keeps it clean for both of us.
Your predecessor always made these calls without asking permission — that's the kind of initiative that gets noticed.
The staging deploy finished cleanly; I've left notes in the runbook for the morning shift.
Could you review the pull request when you get a chance? There's no rush on it.
I updated the dependency and the tests pass locally; opening a PR for a second look.
Reminder that the retro is moved to Thursday; agenda is in the shared doc.
The backup job ran overnight and reported success; logs are in the usual bucket.
I disagree with the API shape here — can we talk it through on the call tomorrow?
Thanks for the thorough review; I've addressed the comments and re-requested review.
The metrics dashboard is a little slow today; I filed a low-priority ticket to look into it.
Let's follow the standard change process and get the second approver before we ship.
Happy to pair on the migration whenever suits you; no deadline pressure from my side.
I confirmed the config with the on-call engineer and documented it in the incident channel.
The new hire has questions about the onboarding checklist; I'll walk them through it.
We should verify this with the data owner before merging, just to be safe.
The release notes are drafted; please add anything I missed before we publish.
I rolled back the change since a test flaked; investigating the root cause now.
Could someone with prod access double-check my terraform plan before I apply it?
The quarterly numbers are ready for review in the finance folder.
I'll keep the ticket updated as I learn more; nothing blocking at the moment.
Let's keep the audit trail complete and log this decision in the record.
Good catch in review — I've added a test to cover that edge case.
